political.s: IBM has a long-standing policy of no direct political contributions and no PAC, presenting itself as politically neutral. The company spent approximately $5.5 million on federal lobbying in 2024. IBM engages in public policy advocacy on issues including technology regulation, cybersecurity, and government procurement, with disclosed lobbying activities.

charity.s: IBM maintains a corporate giving program with matching gifts up to $5,000 per organization and volunteer grant programs. The company emphasizes technology and expertise contributions through initiatives like the IBM Service Corps and SkillsBuild education programs. IBM's philosophy prioritizes offering technology and expertise over direct financial contributions.
charity.focus: Education, technology access, community development, employee volunteering

environment.s: IBM has committed to comprehensive environmental goals including achieving net-zero GHG emissions by 2030, reducing operational emissions 65% by 2025 (target met ahead of schedule in 2023), and procuring 75% renewable electricity by 2025. The company has embedded sustainability across product development, data centers, and supply chain management. IBM leads in developing AI solutions to help clients reduce environmental impact.

labor.s: IBM has faced scrutiny over labor practices, including a $17 million 2026 DOJ settlement over alleged discriminatory DEI practices in federal contracts. The company has experienced unfair labor practice allegations regarding Weingarten rights. However, IBM reports lower attrition among top performers and maintains employee development programs. CEO-to-median employee pay ratio stood at 765:1 in 2025.

dei.s: IBM paid $17 million to settle DOJ allegations in April 2026 that its DEI programs violated anti-discrimination laws by using diversity modifiers in compensation, prioritizing diverse interview slates by race/sex, and tying demographic goals to employment decisions. The company subsequently scaled back DEI initiatives, ending its DEI department and discontinuing participation in HRC's diversity scoring.

animals.s: As a cloud computing and software services company, IBM does not conduct animal testing or have animal-related business practices.

guns.s: IBM Cloud is a software and cloud services platform. The company does not manufacture or sell firearms.

privacy.s: IBM has long emphasized data privacy and security, establishing one of the first corporate privacy codes and appointing an early Chief Privacy Officer. The company complies with GDPR, Data Privacy Framework, and California privacy laws. IBM implements encryption, access controls, and offers transparency reports on law enforcement requests. Privacy is built into product design through Security and Privacy by Design (SPbD@IBM) practices.

execPay.s: IBM CEO Arvind Krishna received $38 million in 2025 compensation (51% increase from prior year), while median employee compensation was approximately $49,630. This reflects a CEO-to-employee pay ratio of approximately 765:1. Executive compensation is heavily weighted toward variable pay (95% for CEO) tied to performance metrics.

sec.note: Pay ratio extracted from proxy filing — verify manually for accuracy
